deep_dive.title: TRIDENT: Breaking the Hybrid-Safety-Physics Coupling for Provably Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Safe coordination in networked cyber-physical systems forces learning algorithms to simultaneously handle hybrid discrete-continuous actions, hard training-time safety constraints, and physics-governed dynamics. We show that these three features form a directed cycle of biases that defeats any naive composition of off-the-shelf modules, and formalize this as a three-way coupling lemma. We then introduce TRIDENT, the first MARL framework whose three components are co-designed to cancel each leak: a Richardson-Romberg gradient correction reducing Gumbel-Softmax bias from O(tau) to O(tau^2), a Lyapunov-constrained sequential trust-region update enforcing per-iterate feasibility, and a physics-informed residual critic that decomposes value rather than reward. We prove an O~(1/sqrt(K)) convergence rate to a constrained Nash equilibrium and an O(sqrt(K)) cumulative-violation bound. On multi-UAV mobile-edge computing, autonomous intersection management, and a hybrid SMAC variant, TRIDENT cuts training-time violations by 95.5% over MADDPG and 76.3% over MACPO, while improving reward by 13.5% over the strongest unconstrained baseline.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多智能体强化学习框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多智能体强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TRIDENT is the first MARL framework that breaks the hybrid-safety-physics coupling using a Richardson-Romberg gradient correction, Lyapunov-constrained trust-region update, and physics-informed residual critic for provably safe coordination.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法无法同时处理混合离散-连续动作、训练时安全约束和物理动态的三重耦合问题。本文提出TRIDENT框架，通过理查森-龙贝格梯度校正降低偏差、李雅普诺夫约束的顺序信任域更新保证每步可行性、物理信息残差评论家准确预测安全风险。实验证明该方法在安全关键任务中显著优于基线，为混合系统提供首个可证明安全的MARL解决方案。

deep_dive.title: R2D-RL: A RoboCup 2D Soccer Environment for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Robot soccer is a challenging testbed for multi-agent reinforcement learning because it combines partial observability, cooperative and adversarial interaction, sparse rewards, and long-horizon tactical behavior. RoboCup 2D Soccer Simulation (RCSS2D) provides a mature robot-soccer platform, but its competition-oriented server-client architecture is difficult to use directly with modern Python-based MARL workflows. We introduce R2D-RL, a reinforcement learning environment that connects RCSS2D and HELIOS-based player clients to a Python MARL interface through shared-memory communication and cycle-level synchronization. R2D-RL supports full-field and scenario-based training with configurable opponents, Base discrete and Hybrid parameterized action spaces, action masks, expected possession value (EPV)-based reward shaping, and parallel execution. We provide front-goal scenarios and an 11-vs-11 full-field benchmark, together with baseline results.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: RoboCup 2D soccer environment for multi-agent reinforcement learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于多智能体强化学习的RoboCup 2D足球环境
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于多智能体强化学习的RoboCup 2D足球环境
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于多智能体强化学习的RoboCup 2D足球环境
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: R2D-RL connects RCSS2D with Python MARL interface via shared memory, enabling full-field and scenario-based training with configurable opponents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对RoboCup 2D足球仿真平台与Python MARL工作流不兼容的问题，开发了R2D-RL环境。通过共享内存和周期级同步将RCSS2D与HELIOS客户端连接，支持全场和场景训练，并提供可配置对手。该环境为多智能体强化学习研究提供了标准化的机器人足球测试床，促进了合作与对抗任务的算法评估。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对RoboCup 2D足球仿真平台与Python MARL工作流不兼容的问题，开发了R2D-RL环境。通过共享内存和周期级同步将RCSS2D与HELIOS客户端连接，支持全场和场景训练，并提供可配置对手。该环境为多智能体强化学习研究提供了标准化的机器人足球测试床，促进了合作与对抗任务的算法评估。

deep_dive.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems (MAS) have demonstrated great potential in solving tasks with execution complexity, by distributing subtasks across cooperative agents. However, this divide-and-conquer paradigm falls short on decision-making tasks that are also prevalent in the real world. These tasks require simultaneous reasoning from the stances of all involved stakeholders whose decisions are mutually dependent and thus cannot be solved in isolation. We characterize this challenge as stance entanglement, a form of decision complexity distinct from execution complexity. To address it, we propose Multi-Agent Fictitious Play (MAFP), a novel MAS paradigm that represents stakeholder stances as agents and formulates decision-making as an equilibrium-seeking process. Built on the game-theoretic principle of fictitious play, MAFP iteratively updates each agent's decision by best responding to the empirical mixture of other agents' past decisions. This enables agents to expose and address one another's weaknesses, progressively improving decision quality and robustness. We evaluate MAFP on challenging decision-making tasks that test the capability of deciding strategies for competitive scenarios prior to acting. MAFP outperforms both single-round and multi-round baselines on two complementary metrics, tournament strength and robustness, demonstrating its effectiveness in addressing stance entanglement.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Multi-agent fictitious play for LLM-based decision-making
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于多智能体虚拟博弈的LLM决策增强
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于多智能体虚拟博弈的LLM决策增强
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于多智能体虚拟博弈的LLM决策增强
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Multi-Agent Fictitious Play (MAFP) for LLM-based multi-agent systems to address stance entanglement in cooperative decision-making tasks, outperforming divide-and-conquer approaches.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于大语言模型的多智能体系统在处理决策任务时面临立场纠缠问题，即各决策方的立场相互依赖而无法孤立求解。本文提出多智能体虚拟博弈（MAFP）范式，将各参与方的立场表示为智能体，并采用虚拟博弈框架进行协同。实验表明MAFP能够有效解决立场纠缠，在多个决策场景中优于传统分治方法。该工作为LLM多智能体系统在复杂决策中的应用提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: The Internet of Agentic AI: Communication, Coordination, and Collective Intelligence at Scale
quick_skim.abstract: The rapid emergence of autonomous AI agents is transforming artificial intelligence from isolated model inference into distributed systems of reasoning, communication, and action. This paper develops the vision of the Internet of Agentic AI (IoAI): an open ecosystem in which heterogeneous agents discover one another, negotiate responsibilities, exchange context, invoke tools, and execute workflows across cloud, edge, device, organizational, and cyber-physical environments. We synthesize foundations from single-agent agentic AI, multi-agent systems, distributed computing, communication networks, game theory, and security engineering to characterize the architectures and mechanisms required for scalable agent ecosystems. The paper examines agent deployment models, workflow lifecycles, communication protocols, interoperability layers, resource-management challenges, and trust architectures, with case studies in adaptive manufacturing and distributed operational coordination. The resulting framework highlights the central research challenges of controlled emergence, semantic interoperability, secure identity, incentive-compatible coordination, resource-aware orchestration, and governance for large-scale networks of autonomous agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: envisions multi-agent systems with communication and coordination
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了多智能体通信与协调的互联网架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出了多智能体通信与协调的互联网架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出了多智能体通信与协调的互联网架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents the Internet of Agentic AI (IoAI) vision for scalable multi-agent ecosystems, synthesizing multi-agent systems, game theory, and distributed computing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前AI代理从独立模型向分布式系统演进，但缺乏统一架构。本文提出IoAI概念，一个开放生态，允许异构代理发现彼此、协商责任、交换上下文。通过融合多智能体系统、博弈论、分布式计算等基础，描述了可扩展代理生态所需的机制。该工作为未来多智能体强化学习的实际部署提供了架构蓝图。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前AI代理从独立模型向分布式系统演进，但缺乏统一架构。本文提出IoAI概念，一个开放生态，允许异构代理发现彼此、协商责任、交换上下文。通过融合多智能体系统、博弈论、分布式计算等基础，描述了可扩展代理生态所需的机制。该工作为未来多智能体强化学习的实际部署提供了架构蓝图。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多智能体强化学习, especially work that connects or combines: multi-agent reinforcement learning; cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning; decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning; centralized training for multi-agent reinforcement learning; decentralized execution in multi-agent systems; Markov games in multi-agent reinforcement learning; multi agent reinforcement learning algorithms; cooperative multi agent reinforcement learning applications; decentralized multi agent reinforcement learning methods; comparison of value decomposition and actor critic methods.

quick_skim.title: Intermittent Strategic Cooperation of Two Selfish Agents on Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: We study strategic space- and time-constrained cooperation between two self-interested agents through the Intermittent Strategic Cooperation-Based Two-Agent Path Planning (IC2PP) problem, a shortest-path game on graphs in which agents navigate toward individual targets while optionally cooperating at specific nodes to reduce their own travel times. Although such cooperation can strictly benefit both agents, it is strategically fragile: agents may deviate at any point along their paths. Modeled as a 2-player game, we characterize the structure of Pure Nash Equilibrium (PNE) joint strategies in IC2PP, and show that stable cooperation must follow a highly constrained form. We further prove that at least one PNE exists in every instance of IC2PP, and present a polynomial-time algorithm for enumerating all relevant PNEs. When multiple equilibria arise, we study coordination mechanisms based on bargaining-theoretic selection concepts and empirically compare equilibrium outcomes in terms of individual travel times and social welfare.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Decentralized cooperation in multi-agent path planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体路径规划中的去中心化合作
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体路径规划中的去中心化合作
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体路径规划中的去中心化合作
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies intermittent strategic cooperation between two selfish agents in graph path planning, characterizing pure Nash equilibrium structure and proving existence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究两个自私智能体在图上进行间歇性战略合作的最短路径博弈问题。通过形式化IC2PP问题，刻画了纯纳什均衡联合策略的结构，并证明每个实例至少存在一个均衡。该工作为理解多智能体系统中不稳定合作的本质提供了理论框架。
